reasoning: The Lean formalization captures the informal statement faithfully. The "increasing sequence of mn+1 positive integers" is encoded as a Finset S of size m*n+1 with all elements positive — Finset enforces distinctness, and since elements are in ℕ the strictly increasing order is recoverable. The antichain condition "no element divides any other" is correctly encoded as ∀ j i ∈ T, i ≠ j → ¬(j ∣ i), which by iterating over both orderings rules out both directions of divisibility between distinct elements. The chain condition "each element (except the greatest) divides the next" is encoded as ∀ i j ∈ T, j < i → j ∣ i; although this literally says every smaller element divides every larger element, this is logically equivalent to consecutive divisibility by transitivity of ∣. The cardinalities (m+1, n+1) and the disjunction structure match. No extraneous hypotheses are added and none are dropped.
